sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) a wing, so called from its covering. בַּעַל כָּנָף Pro 1:17, and בַּעַל הַכְּנָפַיִם Ecc 10:20, that which has a wing, poet. used of a bird. כָּל־כָּנָף allerleh Geflügel, birds of all kinds, Gen 7:14. Poet. there are used, כַּנְפֵי רוּחַ the wings of the wind, Psa 18:11 104:3 כַּנְפֵי שַׁחַר the wings of the morning, 139:9 on account of the rapidity of the moving on of the winds and the morning. Often also used metaph. of care and protection. Psa 17:8, בְּצֵל כְּנָפֶיךָ תַּםְתִּירֵנִי “hide me under the shadow of thy wings.” Psa 36:8 57:2 61:5 63:8 91:4 Rth 2:12. (Compare Arab. جنح, جناح and كنف Schult. on Job, 472; Gr. πτέρυξ, Grot. on Mat 23:37.) Wings are also spoken of as applied to armies (as in Latin) [and English], Isa 8:8 comp. אֲנַפִּים.

(2) edge, extremity-(a) of a garment, the skirt, πτέρυξ, πτερύγιον; fully כְּנַף הַמְּעִיל the skirts of a mantle, 1Sa 24:5, 12 1Sa 24:12 Num 15:38 Deu 22:12 also without the name of the garment, Zec 8:23, כְּנַף אִישׁ יְהוּדִי “the skirt (of a mantle) of a man who is a Jew.” Eze 5:3 Hag 2:12. From the Orientals having been accustomed at night to wrap themselves in their mantles, this expression is used for the edge of a bed covering. Deu 23:1, וְלֹא יְנַלֶּה כְּנַף אָבִיו “neither shall he uncover his father’s coverlet;” i.e. he shall not violate his father’s bed. Deu 27:20 comp. Eze 16:8. Rth 3:9, “spread thy coverlet over thy handmaid;” i.e. take me to thy couch as thy wife. (Comp. Theoc. Idyll. xviii. 19, and ἐπισκιάζειν, Luk 1:35 [?]).-(b) the extremities of the earth (just as the inhabited earth is often compared to a cloak spread out). Isa 24:16, כְּנַף הָאָרֶץ “the extremity of the earth.” Especially in pl. Job 37:3 38:13 , כַּנְפֹות הָאָרֶץ “the extremities of the earth;” and Isa 11:12. Eze 7:2, אַדְבַּעַת כַּנְפֹות הָאָרֶץ “the four quarters,” or “extreme bounds of the earth.”-(c) the highest summit of the temple, Dan 9:27 comp. πτερύγιον τοῦ ἱεροῦ, Mat 4:5.

Dual כְּנָפַיִם const. כַּנְפֵי fem. pr. a pair of wings; hence often used for the pl. שֵׁשׁ כְּנָפַיִם six wings, Isa 6:2 אַדְבַּע כְּנָפַיִם four wings, Eze 1:6 10:21 . Pl. const. כַּנְפֹות m. used of the skirts of a garment, Deu 22:12 and of the extremity of the earth (see No. 2, a, b).
